prevalence.source: SBH - Sociedade Brasileira de Hepatologia / DataSUS
prevalence.trend: increasing due to rising NAFLD and obesity rates

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Fibrosis staging via FIB-4, transient elastography, biopsy per SBH

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Hepatitis screening via SUS, limited NAFLD screening
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 45% due to improved viral hepatitis management but gaps in NAFLD

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SBH guidelines, elastography when available, viral markers via SUS
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hepatitis B/C treatment via SUS (tenofovir, DAAs), alcohol cessation programs, NAFLD lifestyle intervention
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising NAFLD/NASH due to obesity epidemic, alcohol consumption, limited elastography access
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Universal hepatitis C DAA treatment via SUS, viral hepatitis vaccination program
